seoTitle: How to Fix Wix Error 404 Page Not Found
metaDescription: Learn how to diagnose broken links causing 404 errors on Wix and set up 301 redirects to protect your search rankings.
intro: A 404 error means a visitor landed on a URL that no longer exists, which can hurt both user experience and your search rankings if left unfixed. This guide covers tracking down the source of the broken link, setting up permanent redirects, and building a helpful custom 404 page as a safety net.

steps.heading: Identify the broken pages
steps.body: Find which URLs are triggering the 404 error, often through analytics or a broken link report.

steps.heading: Check your URL slugs
steps.body: Review the Pages and Menu editor to verify correct URL slug syntax for the pages in question.

steps.heading: Open the URL Redirect Manager
steps.body: Navigate to the advanced SEO tools dashboard inside Marketing & SEO and launch the Redirect Manager.

steps.heading: Create a 301 redirect
steps.body: Enter the old broken URL and map it to your target active destination page.

steps.heading: Save and activate the redirect
steps.body: Save the mapping to activate the permanent redirect rule instantly.

steps.heading: Build a custom 404 page
steps.body: Design a helpful 404 landing page with working navigation links so visitors don't simply bounce off a dead end.

faq.question: What causes a 404 error on Wix?
faq.answer: It's usually a deleted page, a mistyped URL slug, or an old link pointing to a page that no longer exists.

faq.question: Does a 404 error hurt my SEO?
faq.answer: A lingering 404 can hurt user experience and potentially search ranking value, which is why setting up a 301 redirect is recommended for permanently removed pages.

faq.question: What's the difference between a redirect and a custom 404 page?
faq.answer: A redirect automatically sends visitors from the old URL to a working page, while a custom 404 page is a fallback shown when no redirect exists.

faq.question: How do I verify Google isn't still showing the broken link?
faq.answer: Use Google Search Console to check indexed URLs and confirm the old broken link has been cleared or redirected properly.
